text: Hey, I want to give you honest feedback on your short story. The pacing in the middle drags a bit, and some of the dialogue feels forced. I know that's hard to hear, but I think you can make it really strong.

I've been looking at market rates and my contributions this year. I'd like to discuss adjusting my salary to 95k. I understand budgets are tight, but I think it's fair based on the value I'm bringing.
I noticed you mentioned my 'enthusiasm' in the meeting, and it felt a little backhanded. If there's something specific about my approach that bothers you, I'd rather hear it directly.
Mom, I love you, but I need you to stop calling me three times a day. I'll check in every other day, but I need space to focus on my own stuff right now.
Look, I'm sorry I snapped at you about the dishes. You didn't deserve that. But I also think it's not fair that I'm the only one who ever loads the dishwasher.
Hey, it's been a few months since I lent you that 300 bucks for your car repair. I don't want to make it weird, but could we set up a plan to pay it back?
I really appreciate you sharing all that about your divorce, but I'm not the right person to be your therapist. Maybe talking to a professional would help more.
I can't cover your shift every weekend. I have my own life and commitments. I'm happy to swap occasionally, but this needs to be more balanced.
I know you think your project idea is the best, but you've interrupted everyone else three times now. Can we let Sarah finish her pitch before we jump in?
That comment about my presentation skills felt like a dig. If you have real feedback, I can handle it, but let's drop the sugarcoating.

Hey, I've been meaning to bring this up for a while. It bothers me when you leave your dirty dishes in the sink overnight. I know it's small, but it's been building up and I'd really appreciate if we could both try to clean as we go.
I noticed you presented my analysis in the team meeting as your own. I'm glad it helped, but I need you to credit me next time—or at least loop me in before sharing my work.
Why are you still single, though? You're so great. I just don't get it.
So, for the dinner on Saturday, I know you two aren't speaking. I don't expect you to be best friends, but can you at least be civil? I don't want everyone else to feel awkward.
I was wrong. I'm sorry I raised my voice in the argument. I got defensive and said things I regret. Let me try again.
Hi Professor, I know I got a B on the final paper, but I was wondering if you could round it up to an A? I worked really hard and I think the content was solid overall.
Can you cover my shift every Saturday for the next month? I know it's a lot to ask, but my family situation is really complicated right now.
Wow, you actually look great in that dress. It really hides your midsection well.
I appreciate you sharing all that about your divorce, but I'm not really the right person to vent to about this. Maybe you could talk to a therapist?
I know you didn't mean it that way, but when you said 'typical Lisa' in front of everyone, it felt like you were dismissing my idea. Can we talk about it?
